# config.template chroma database leaf: make declarative, relocate UNIT_TEST_MODE override to test env

## Context

`ai/config.template.mjs` `engines.chroma.database` is one of the four inline-`process.env` leaf defaults baselined by the SSOT lint (#12474, now on dev):

```js
database: leaf(process.env.UNIT_TEST_MODE === 'true' ? CHROMA_TEST_DATABASE : CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE, 'NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE', 'string')
```

This is the **clean, fail-closed static case** of the #12451 declarative-reshape half — the lint half landed (#12474); this leaf is the first reshape burndown.

## The Fix

Make the leaf declarative — `leaf(CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE, 'NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE', 'string')` — and relocate the test override to the **`test-unit` npm script shell env** (`NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE=neo-unit-test`, the reliable worker-reaching channel; `playwright.config.unit.mjs` in-process mutation does not reliably reach workers, which is why `npm run test-unit` is mandated). Drop the now-reshaped `NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE` row from the lint `BASELINE` (else the stale-baseline check fails).

## Safety (why this is the safe one)

**Fail-closed.** `ChromaManager.mjs:120` refuses `CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE` under `UNIT_TEST_MODE` — so a missed/wrong relocation fails **loud** (refuses the prod DB), never silently pollutes production. (The graph `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH` case lacks an equivalent guard → deferred to its own leaf; the dynamic collection-name cases need a per-worker bootstrap → also deferred.)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `engines.chroma.database` leaf is declarative (no inline `process.env`); env override via the `NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE` arg.
- [ ] `test-unit` npm script sets `NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE=neo-unit-test`; unit suite resolves the test database.
- [ ] The `NEO_CHROMA_DATABASE` row is removed from the SSOT-lint `BASELINE` (lint stays green: 3 baselined, no new violation, no stale row).
- [ ] Tenant/store isolation unaffected (the `ChromaManager` fail-closed guard is the by-construction backstop).

## Scope

Chroma database leaf only. The graph (`NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH`) static case + the dynamic collection-name cases remain on #12451 (their own follow-ups — distinct risk/complexity).

Refs #12451 (reshape umbrella). Part of Epic #12456 (AiConfig reactive Provider SSOT cleanup).

Origin Session ID: 3ecb40bf-bfef-40b1-8693-a8aae5afa1b7

- 2026-06-04T08:41:21Z @neo-claude-opus referenced in commit `f1c73a7` - "refactor(ai): config.template chroma leaf uses inline literal, not an imported constant (#12480)

Operator correction: config.template IS the SSOT — it must define its values inline, never import them. The prior reshape swapped the inline UNIT_TEST_MODE branch for an imported CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE constant — still 'config depending on an external var', the same antipattern. Use the literal 'default_database'; drop the chromaTestIsolation import entirely (config.template now imports zero config values).

chromaTestIsolation.mjs keeps its CHROMA_PRODUCTION_DATABASE constant as an INDEPENDENT defense-in-depth guard invariant — the ChromaManager fail-closed guard must not trust config (that is the point of the guard), so this is intentional, not a config-value duplication. 13/13 specs pass (config.template resolution + ChromaManager isolation + the fail-closed guard); lint green at 3 baselined.

Co-Authored-By: Neo Claude Opus <neo-claude-opus@neomjs.com>"
